column6: mojo_behavior
column7: mojito_behavior
column9: notes

column6: Indentation delimits suites and continuation rules suppress logical newlines.
column7: Lexer implements indentation, dedentation, continuations, comments, and bracket suppression.

column6: Supports integer, floating, boolean, raw/multiline/adjacent string, and interpolated literal families; Byte is an alias of UInt8, not a byte-string syntax.
column7: Lexes and runs current numeric spellings, Byte/UInt8 values, adjacent ordinary or t-string families, and nested lexical interpolation boundaries.
column9: Exact arbitrary-precision numeric evaluation and lazy/self-hosted string representation are semantic type/library work recorded below, not missing literal syntax.

column6: Identifiers include keyword stropping for names that would otherwise be reserved.
column7: Identifiers and stropped names parse, check, mangle, and execute.

column6: Runtime statements are forbidden at file scope; declarations and compile-time bindings are allowed.
column7: Production compilation rejects executable file-scope statements.
column9: Legacy phase tests may opt into a non-conforming snippet mode.

column6: `def` declares functions and methods with typed arguments and optional result types.
column7: Ordinary `def` functions and methods check and execute.

column6: The removed legacy `fn` keyword is rejected; `def` is the sole function-declaration keyword.
column7: Legacy `fn` declarations are rejected by the parser.
column9: Compatibility is rejection parity, not an implementation target.

column6: Supports positional-only, keyword-only, default, and keyword arguments.
column7: Shared call binding supports `/`, `*`, defaults, and keywords.

column6: Homogeneous variadic positional and keyword collectors bind repeated values; an owned keyword dictionary forwards with `**kwargs^`.
column7: Homogeneous `*args` and `**kwargs` run across free, generic, instance, static, and bounded-trait calls; collectors are owned insertion-ordered `StringDict[T]` values, and consuming forwarding reuses the shared binder and effect/origin checks.
column9: The VM transports unmatched keyword pairs in an internal ordered ABI before constructing the source-visible collector.

column6: Type packs permit heterogeneous variadic arguments with per-element types and compile-time iteration.
column7: Generic `*args: *ArgTypes` declarations infer statically evident expression types, check every element against its pack bound, expose concrete per-index types to compile-time iteration, and execute through a type-erased collection.
column9: Because specialization precedes body checking, pack inference is syntactic for expressions not already carrying checked types.

column6: Ordinary returns transfer an owned value and may apply implicit conversion.
column7: Typed owned returns, literal coercion, recursion, and path checking run.
column9: Return checking is intentionally conservative.

column6: Reference returns carry origins tying the result to caller-owned storage.
column7: Origin-checked reference returns, unions, projections, and escape rejection run.
column9: Safe caller-owned subset.

column6: Named `out` results support caller-transparent direct construction of nonmovable values.
column7: A single named result is excluded from caller argument binding, checked for initialization, and returned directly; constructor `out self` also runs.
column9: Definite initialization is conservative pending lifecycle-flow completion.

column6: Overload selection minimizes implicit conversions, applies contextual default types to otherwise-unconstrained literals, then prefers nonvariadic candidates, shorter signatures, and applicable specialization tie-breaks.
column7: Functions, methods, and constructors rank after contextual generic-target substitution; unconstrained integer and floating literals prefer `Int` and `Float64`, while genuinely equivalent conversion paths remain ambiguous.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Struct methods may be declared static and called without a receiver.
column7: User-defined static methods check, overload, lower, and execute through the shared default/keyword call ABI without synthesizing a receiver.
column9: Shared call binding applies equally to static methods.

column6: Functions have signature types; current Mojo specializes generic functions through compile-time aliases before using them as callables.
column7: Function values inhabit typed variables, parameters, and fields; expected signatures select overloaded candidates and contextually specialize generics while preserving raising effects. Nominal def(...) conforming structs dispatch through __call__.
column9: Mojito materializes generic specialization as a runtime function value where current Mojo commonly uses a compile-time alias.

column6: `var` supports explicit or inferred types and later assignment preserves type.
column7: Typed, inferred, and var-less introductions check and run with stable binding identity.

column6: Typed variables may be declared uninitialized and must be definitely initialized before use.
column7: Typed late declarations run; path joins require initialization on every reaching branch, and reads before complete initialization reject.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Implicitly declared variables have function scope while explicit `var` bindings have block scope.
column7: Nested var-less introductions receive stable function-owned identities and remain visible after their block; reachable-path joins reject reads unless every reaching path initialized the binding.
column9: Loop analysis conservatively retains the zero-iteration path.

column6: Bindings use lexical scope and may shadow in nested scopes.
column7: Lexical scopes, same-scope redeclaration errors, and stable IDs under shadowing run.

column6: Standard scalar structs include sized integers, floating types, Bool, and SIMD width-one aliases; Int aliases Scalar[DType.int].
column7: Core Int/Scalar[DType.int] identity, UInt, Bool, Float64, sized numeric aliases, wrapping, and conversions run.
column9: The full dtype and literal-conversion matrix is incomplete.

column6: String and string-slice APIs provide Unicode-aware storage, formatting, and protocol integration.
column7: VM strings support literals, concatenation, equality, membership, length, and pragmatic conversion.
column9: No self-hosted Unicode-complete String.

column6: String literals include raw, multiline, adjacent, and lazy interpolated TString forms.
column7: Raw, multiline, same-line/indented adjacent, and adjacent t-string forms run with Writable-checked interpolation; the VM currently materializes them eagerly as String.
column9: Lazy self-hosted TString and StringLiteral materialization remain in Complete MIR-Schema-Prerequisite CPU Semantics.

column6: Heterogeneous tuples support displays, typed construction, compile-time indexing, unpacking, packs, comparison, and consuming utility operations.
column7: Bare displays/destructuring, inferred and typed construction, compile-time indexing, length, membership, structural equality/order, concat, reverse, coercion, value semantics, and `Tuple[*Ts](*args^)` pack round-tripping run. Explicit transfers into reverse/concat are tracked as consuming moves.
column9: Runtime heterogeneous indexing and pack spreading into arbitrary fixed-arity calls are rejected by current Mojo and are not parity targets.

column6: Standard List, Dict, Set, Optional, and related generic collections are available.
column7: List literals plus bundled self-hosted List, Dict, Set, HashDict, HashSet, and Optional proof subsets run.
column9: Not API-compatible with the full Mojo standard library.

column6: List, set, and dictionary displays plus comprehensions construct inferred collection types.
column7: Homogeneous list/set/dictionary displays and nested/filtering CPU comprehensions check inference, hashing, ownership, and source-order evaluation, then lower to explicit collection-construction MIR.
column9: Builtin collection values have VM-native storage; general user-protocol dispatch remains in Complete MIR-Schema-Prerequisite CPU Semantics.

column6: Slice syntax selects `ContiguousSlice` or `StridedSlice`, preserves optional start/end/step bounds, and supports mixed or variadic get/set protocols; standard collections define result/view semantics.
column7: Omitted, negative, and strided bounds normalize through `indices()`; checked descriptor overloads and mixed/variadic `__getitem__`/`__setitem__` execute. Built-in List and String still use Mojito's eager VM results.
column9: Language-level user slicing is complete; Span-returning List slices and Unicode String/StringSlice APIs remain in the CPU semantic and standard-library milestones.

column6: `std.utils.Variant` stores one of a compile-time list of types and exposes membership queries, tag tests, reference projection, mutation, consuming extraction, replacement, and conditional protocols.
column7: Explicit type packs, imported construction, `is_type_supported[T]()`, `isa[T]()`, typed projection, `set[T](value)`, checked/unsafe `take[T]()`, checked/unsafe `replace[Tin, Tout](value)`, and element-wise lifecycle/Hashable/Writable/Equatable gating run through checked/MIR operations.
column9: The VM gives unsafe operations deterministic unchecked-tag behavior rather than host undefined behavior. `destroy_with`, representation writing, and the fully generic TypeList-driven library surface remain in Grow The CPU Standard Library. Pattern matching is not yet a published current-Mojo Variant feature.

column6: Operator syntax dispatches through dunder methods and standard numeric protocols.
column7: Arithmetic, comparison, boolean, membership, unary, power, floor division, augmented, bitwise, shift, and matrix-multiply syntax runs for builtins or checked user protocols.
column9: Full standard numeric protocolization remains in Complete MIR-Schema-Prerequisite CPU Semantics.

column6: Comparisons chain and short-circuit while evaluating operands in order.
column7: Chained comparisons and boolean short-circuiting run.

column6: `if`/`elif`/`else` and conditional expressions branch on Boolable conditions.
column7: Statements and ternary expressions lower through HIR/MIR and run.
column9: Builtin Boolable coverage is narrower than the full stdlib.

column6: `while` and protocol-driven `for` loops support break, continue, immutable and reference bindings, and consuming owned iteration.
column7: While, range/list/user iteration, break/continue, `for ref` write-through, and `for var item in collection^` run; the transferred source is moved once and residual elements are destroyed on early exit when permitted.
column9: Owned builtin collections use the VM iterator state; general collection protocolization remains later schema-prerequisite work.

column6: `while` and `for` may run an `else` suite after normal exhaustion but not after break or return.
column7: HIR/MIR preserve loop `else`; normal exhaustion executes it and `break` bypasses it for both loop forms.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Destructuring and pattern forms bind structured values.
column7: Tuple declaration destructuring and simultaneous tuple assignment run with checked arity and value flow.
column9: General pattern matching remains unavailable; no speculative future Variant pattern syntax is claimed.

column6: Errors propagate through `raise` and are handled by try/except/else/finally.
column7: Builtin and user-defined typed error values propagate through VM frames and structured handlers without losing their fields.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Raising functions, trait requirements, and calls participate in checked `raises` effects.
column7: `raise` and selected direct, indirect, method, trait-requirement, and bounded-dispatch calls carry exact typed error contracts through checked HIR into MIR and must be handled or propagated.
column9: A nonraising implementation may satisfy a raising requirement; a raising implementation may not widen its declared error family.

column6: Errors may be typed and parameterized, including Never interactions.
column7: Typed errors, inferred handler bindings, parametric callable errors, `Never` bottom coercion, and nonraising `raises Never` are checked.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Context managers use `with`, `__enter__`, and `__exit__` protocols.
column7: Checked enter/exit calls execute around the body, including raising exits and mutation/write-back through the manager receiver.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Structs define stored fields, methods, parameters, and declared trait conformance.
column7: Fields, methods, generic parameters, associated facts, and nominal conformance run.

column6: Struct values are independent under copy and transfer under move.
column7: Struct copy/move behavior and nested value semantics run.

column6: Source modules and packages use explicit relative imports; dotted absolute imports bind every prefix; resolution prefers source packages, `.mojoc`, source modules, then legacy `.mojopkg`.
column7: Selective, wildcard, dotted, dots-only relative, aliased, and block-scoped source imports link with declaration provenance and lexical shadowing. Ordinary directories form namespaces, every dotted prefix binds, package members require explicit import/re-export, and source packages beat source modules.
column9: The complete ordering awaits versioned `.mojoc` and legacy `.mojopkg` loading in Packaging, Artifacts, And Developer Tooling; the source-only choices match.

column6: `import module` and aliases create qualified module namespaces while declarations in different modules retain distinct identity.
column7: Aliased and unaliased dotted qualified calls, values, and types resolve to module-qualified internal symbols, isolating same-named declarations and overloads.
column9: General first-class module values remain outside the flattened architecture.

column6: Argument conventions distinguish preferred `imm` (legacy `read`), mut, var, ref, out, and deinit access with access rules based on transfer, copying, and aliasing.
column7: Mojito accepts `imm` and temporary `read` as the same immutable convention; `var`, `mut`, `ref`, `out`, and `deinit` semantics run.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Ownership transfer, use-after-move prevention, partial moves, and reinitialization are checked.
column7: CFG ownership analysis covers moves, partial moves, branches, loops, calls, and reinitialization.

column6: Borrowed references extend owner lifetime and enforce shared-versus-exclusive access.
column7: Persistent field-sensitive CFG loans cover local and cross-call reference paths through last use.
column9: Safe caller-owned subset.

column6: Static, untracked, unsafe, inferred, union, and projected origins describe reference provenance.
column7: Static, untracked, unsafe-any, named, inferred, union, and projected contracts lower into the checked origin algebra; UnsafePointer(to=place) infers concrete place origins, and reference- or pointer-storing aggregates preserve handles and executable owner loans.
column9: Pointer loans enforce reference-style exclusivity, and arithmetic, comparison, and free() on origin-bearing pointers are rejected; type-level origin instantiation and origin-generic pointer calls remain deferred.

column6: Closures use explicit capture lists and cannot outlive their defining scope.
column7: Unified imm/read, mut, moved, and default captures lower to frame/slot environments; sibling forwarding, recursion, generics, closure values, and persistent mutation run, while undeclared capture and escape are rejected.
column9: Escaping closures are intentionally rejected.

column6: Initializers, named results, and definite initialization establish every field before normal return.
column7: Fieldwise and handwritten construction, named results, raising initializers, and branch/exception-sensitive out-self definite initialization run.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Unified `__init__(..., copy=)` and `__init__(..., move=)` methods control explicit copying and transfer.
column7: Both unified initializer shapes drive copyability, move behavior, and value semantics through normalized lifecycle symbols.
column9: Legacy initializer spellings are still accepted as a compatibility extension pending source migration.

column6: ImplicitlyDeletable conformance controls automatic deletion; conditional nonconformance makes a value linear, while @explicit_destroy only adds a required diagnostic message.
column7: Mojito derives linearity from explicitly unavailable ImplicitlyDeletable conformance and enforces path- and field-sensitive obligations, projected destruction, reconstruction, CFG joins, and raising fallback paths.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: `comptime` bindings evaluate compile-time values usable by specialization.
column7: Compile-time Int/type facts and materialized constants run.
column9: Value shapes are narrower.

column6: `comptime if` selects branches and `comptime for` unrolls compile-time iterables.
column7: Both forms elaborate before checking with fuel and unroll limits.
column9: Supported compile-time value shapes are narrower.

column6: Parameters accept types, values, origins, dependent expressions, and variadic packs.
column7: Typed scalar/aggregate values, type/value defaults, positional/named/inferred/infer-only binding, dependent defaults, and heterogeneous packs run; origins remain semantic-only.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Traits declare required methods and compile-time members; structs declare nominal conformance.
column7: Required methods, receiver conventions, nominal conformance, and associated facts run.

column6: Traits refine other traits and may supply inherited default implementations.
column7: Refined requirements and capabilities are inherited; default bodies are statically materialized into conforming structs, with explicit struct methods overriding defaults and ambiguity rejected.
column9: Static materialization avoids runtime trait-object dispatch.

column6: Traits expose associated types and values used by generic code.
column7: Associated compile-time type/value facts compose bounds across refined traits, preserve one associated identity, and diagnose kind/type conflicts.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Parameterized structs may conform conditionally based on parameter capabilities.
column7: Per-conformance `where` predicates support `conforms_to`, boolean composition, and typed value comparisons after specialization; the same constraint algebra serves declarations and methods.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Generic functions, methods, and structs specialize for concrete type/value arguments.
column7: Type parameters erase in runtime MIR; typed values and packs participate in structural identity; dependent defaults, recursive requests, deduplication, shared fuel, and source-located quota failures run.
column9: The VM erases runtime type arguments after static specialization.

column6: Constraints use trailing where clauses; parameter-list where was removed. Type equality uses ==/!= and conforms_to accepts variadic type-list operands.
column7: Mojito rejects the removed placement and solves trailing boolean/value/type predicates, scalar and pack-wide conforms_to, conditional methods, and conditional conformance.
column9: No known first-pass divergence.

column6: Compile-time values materialize into runtime values under defined conversions.
column7: Int, UInt, Float64, Bool, String, tuple, and list values materialize recursively; type, symbolic-parameter, origin, and reflection handles erase before checking.
column9: Compile-time-only facts are erased rather than represented by runtime VM values.

column6: Reflection exposes chainable reflect[T].field[name] and field_at[index] handles whose type is available as .T.
column7: Mojito returns zero-sized reflected handles from named and indexed selection, composes selection across nested structs, substitutes generic field types, and rejects the removed field_type spelling.
column9: ABI byte offsets remain native-backend facts rather than VM reflection values.

column6: UnsafePointer supports typed allocation, load/store, arithmetic, provenance, alignment, and explicit deallocation.
column7: The VM uses stable allocation identities and element offsets, bounds each allocation, supports aligned allocation, arithmetic, subtraction/equality, dangling placeholders, and real invalidating deallocation; place-origin pointers execute as frame/slot handles whose static loans are erased only by the VM value representation.
column9: The VM diagnoses unsafe memory violations deterministically instead of permitting host undefined behavior.

column6: SIMD values expose vector lane semantics and lower to CPU vector operations where available.
column7: The VM emulates typed lanes serially with SIMDSize compile-time widths, `_` construction inference, access, mutation, splats, arithmetic, and comparison.
column9: Semantic subset is intended to match; there is no hardware vector lowering.

column6: Indexer converts index values through `__mlir_index__`; collection access uses concrete indexing APIs.
column7: Int and user Indexer values normalize for VM collection/pointer indexing, while concrete and opaque-bound `__getitem__`/`__setitem__` dispatch remains available.
column9: The VM represents the compiler's MLIR index result as Int.

column6: `print`, String conversion, repr, and text formatting use Writable and Writer; deprecated Stringable/Representable are not protocol targets.
column7: Writable reflective defaults and display/repr overrides stream heterogeneous values through Writer; String fields support manual/automatic selection, repr, escaping, alignment, width, and float precision.
column9: The VM models StringSlice writes with validated String values and implements a focused format-spec subset.

column6: Hashable and Hasher protocols support deterministic and incremental hashing contracts.
column7: Hashable contributes to a caller-provided existential Hasher; update accepts checked Hashable values, reflective defaults hash fields, and bundled IncrementalHasher exposes initialization, update, and finish.
column9: The target-independent VM represents the standard UInt64 result with its word-sized UInt.

column6: Layout and LayoutTensor describe CPU/GPU memory layouts and layout-aware access.
column7: No general layout type or LayoutTensor semantics.
column9: GPU-specific portions are excluded; CPU layout semantics remain first-pass work.

column6: GPU kernels, memory spaces, synchronization, and TileTensor execute on accelerators.
column7: GPU execution is intentionally outside first-pass parity.

column6: The language and libraries expose concurrency, parallelism, atomics, tasks, and distributed execution.
column7: Concurrency, parallelism, atomics, tasks, and distributed execution are intentionally excluded.

column6: Mojo imports Python, exposes Mojo to Python, and converts Python object types.
column7: Python interoperability is not a Mojito goal.

column6: Mojo builds and runs native artifacts for configured compilation targets.
column7: Mojito runs checked MIR in a register VM and has no native artifact backend yet.
column9: Cranelift and LLVM are ordered roadmap work.

column6: Compiler feature toggles select experimental language capabilities.
column7: No compatible feature-toggle system.

column6: Mojo provides source debugging and diagnostic tooling.
column7: Mojito provides staged CLI diagnostics but no source debugger.

column6: Mojo includes language-aware test tooling and standard test APIs.
column7: Mojito has Rust and fixture conformance tests but no self-hosted testing package.

column6: Mojo packages and module artifacts support distribution and dependency workflows.
column7: Mojito links source modules from search roots; package artifacts and dependency metadata are open.

column6: Mojo has compiler-owned IR tooling; its internal format is not Mojito's contract.
column7: A stable Mojito-owned textual MIR/VM assembly format is planned but absent.
column9: Required before native backends.

column6: Mojo emits optimized native CPU code through its compiler stack.
column7: Cranelift is the planned first Mojito native CPU backend.

column6: Mojo emits optimized native CPU code through its compiler stack.
column7: LLVM is the planned second Mojito native CPU backend.

column6: No core Mojo parity requirement.
column7: Optional Mojito backend investigation after native CPU backends.

column6: Mojo is implemented on MLIR.
